prompt:
Find all complex numbers $z$ such that
$$ \mathrm{Re}(z) = 9 \quad \text{and} \quad \mathrm{Im}(z^2) = \mathrm{Im}(z^3). \qquad (\text{AIME 2007}) $$
response_text:
Let z = 9 + bi where b is real.

z² = (9 + bi)² = 81 - b² + 18bi, so Im(z²) = 18b

z³ = z · z² = (9 + bi)(81 - b² + 18bi) = 9(81 - b²) + 9·18bi + 81bi - b²·bi + 18b·i²·b... let me compute carefully.

z³ = (9 + bi)(81 - b² + 18bi)
= 9(81 - b²) + 9(18bi) + bi(81 - b²) + bi(18bi)
= 729 - 9b² + 162bi + 81bi - b³i + 18b²i²
= 729 - 9b² - 18b² + (162b + 81b - b³)i
= 729 - 27b² + (243b - b³)i

So Im(z³) = 243b - b³

Setting Im(z²) = Im(z³):
18b = 243b - b³
b³ = 225b
b(b² - 225) = 0
b = 0, b = 15, b = -15

The solutions are z = 9, z = 9 + 15i, z = 9 - 15i.

Final answer: $z \in \{9,\ 9+15i,\ 9-15i\}$
